### File Overview
This file implements a JSON FFI engine wrapper around the internal threaded model engine, exposing methods to initialize, reload, and send chat/completion requests and to stream JSON responses back to the caller. It constructs JSON response chunks for streaming and final error/usage responses, but it does not perform token-level logprob/top_logprobs merging itself.

### Architecture
- JSONFFIEngine is the base class providing public methods to submit and manage requests (ChatCompletion, Abort, GetLastError, ExitBackgroundLoop) and to stream back error/usage blocks.
- JSONFFIEngineImpl inherits from JSONFFIEngine and tvm::runtime::ModuleNode, registers a TVM module vtable and implements engine lifecycle methods (InitBackgroundEngine, Reload, Unload, Reset, RunBackgroundLoop, RunBackgroundStreamBackLoop) plus the core response builder GetResponseFromStreamOutput.
- The threaded model engine instance is held in engine_ (created in JSONFFIEngine constructor). JSONFFIEngineImpl initializes the engine with a request_stream_callback that wraps GetResponseFromStreamOutput. The engine produces Array<RequestStreamOutput> deltas that GetResponseFromStreamOutput turns into serialized JSON strings which are passed to the original Python/FFI callback.
- Response construction and serialization use picojson and the ChatCompletion* helper types (ChatCompletionRequest, ChatCompletionStreamResponse, ChatCompletionStreamResponseChoice, ChatCompletionMessage, etc.). Tokenization/streaming of token ids to text is delegated to a TextStreamer instance stored per-choice in RequestState (accessed here via rstate.streamer).

### Functions & Classes
- JSONFFIEngine() — constructor. Creates the serve::ThreadedEngine instance (engine_). Important as it sets up the underlying engine used for all requests.
- bool ChatCompletion(std::string request_json_str, std::string request_id) — public method to submit a chat/completion request. Calls AddRequest and, on failure, calls StreamBackError to immediately stream an error response. Critical entrypoint for user requests via the FFI.
- void StreamBackError(std::string request_id) — constructs and streams back a JSON error response for a request. It:
  - Builds a ChatCompletionMessage (delta) with role "assistant" and content err_.
  - Wraps that in a ChatCompletionStreamResponseChoice with finish_reason = FinishReason::error.
  - Builds ChatCompletionStreamResponse (response) with id = request_id, model = "json_ffi", and choices containing the error choice.
  - Serializes the response into a JSON array and also appends a final usage block (prompt_tokens/completion_tokens/total_tokens set to 0) as a second element in the array.
  - Calls request_stream_callback_ with the serialized array.
  Importance: Guarantees the invariant that an error response is streamed followed by a final usage block, matching consumer expectations for streaming output.
- bool AddRequest(std::string request_json_str, std::string request_id) — parses incoming request JSON to ChatCompletionRequest, constructs prompt inputs (via CreatePrompt) unless a special debug request, builds a GenerationConfigNode from request fields (including setting gen_cfg->logprobs and gen_cfg->top_logprobs), validates the GenerationConfig, constructs a Request and RequestState, prepares per-choice TextStreamer instances, stores RequestState in request_map_, and forwards the Request to engine_->AddRequest.
  Importance: Translates incoming JSON request into engine-level request representation and propagation of generation settings (notably request.logprobs and request.top_logprobs are copied into gen_cfg).
  Note: Although it sets gen_cfg->logprobs and gen_cfg->top_logprobs, this file does not implement token-level logprob/top_logprobs handling or merging — those would be produced downstream by the engine or streamer.
- bool Abort(std::string request_id) — calls engine_->AbortRequest(request_id) and erases local request state from request_map_. Important for request cancellation.
- std::string GetLastError() — returns err_. Simple accessor for last error string.
- void ExitBackgroundLoop() — delegates to engine_->ExitBackgroundLoop(). Used to stop engine threads.
- ~JSONFFIEngine() — destructor that calls ExitBackgroundLoop() to clean up background work.
- class JSONFFIEngineImpl : public JSONFFIEngine, public ModuleNode — concrete module exposed to TVM/FFI with registered vtable entries:
  - TVM_MODULE_VTABLE_BEGIN/END and TVM_MODULE_VTABLE_ENTRY(...) — register mapping from exported function names (like "init_background_engine", "chat_completion", etc.) to the corresponding JSONFFIEngineImpl methods.
  - void InitBackgroundEngine(int device_type, int device_id, Optional<PackedFunc> request_stream_callback) — initializes device, stores request_stream_callback_, wraps an FFI request_stream_callback (frequest_stream_callback_wrapper) that receives Array<RequestStreamOutput> and calls GetResponseFromStreamOutput to convert them to a JSON string, and then calls engine_->InitThreadedEngine with the wrapper callback. Importance: Hooks engine-produced RequestStreamOutput deltas into the JSON response builder in this file.
  - void Reload(String engine_config_json_str) — reloads engine config, updates default_generation_config_, loads model config, conversation template (conv_template_), model_config_, and tokenizer_. Importance: populates conv_template_ and model_config_ used by AddRequest/CreatePrompt and sets tokenizer_ used by TextStreamer.
  - void Unload() — calls engine_->Unload(). Lifecycle operation.
  - void Reset() — calls engine_->Reset(). Lifecycle operation.
  - void RunBackgroundLoop() — calls engine_->RunBackgroundLoop(). Lifecycle operation.
  - void RunBackgroundStreamBackLoop() — calls engine_->RunBackgroundStreamBackLoop(). Lifecycle operation.
  - String GetResponseFromStreamOutput(Array<RequestStreamOutput> delta_outputs) — core builder that converts engine produced streaming deltas into JSON response chunks:
    - Iterates delta_outputs.
    - For each delta_output, finds RequestState by request_id in request_map_.
    - If delta_output->request_final_usage_json_str.defined(), it parses that JSON, sets response.usage and pushes a single final ChatCompletionStreamResponse JSON element; erases the request state and continues.
    - Otherwise, verifies arrays sizes (group_finish_reason and group_delta_token_ids) and that they match rstate.streamer.size().
    - For each choice i in the group:
      - Builds ChatCompletionStreamResponseChoice choice, maps optional finish_reason string into FinishReason enum (stop/length/tool_calls/error).
      - Converts delta_token_ids (IntTuple) to std::vector<int32_t> and calls rstate.streamer[i]->Put(delta_token_ids_vec) to get textual content; if finish_reason defined, append rstate.streamer[i]->Finish() to content.
      - If content non-empty or finish_reason present, sets ChatCompletionMessage delta with role="assistant" and content, and pushes the choice into response.choices.
    - If response.choices is non-empty, pushes response.AsJSON() into the output array.
    - Serializes the accumulated array via picojson::value(json_response_arr).serialize() and returns the string.
    Importance: This is the function that constructs the JSON chunks sent to the external callback. It maps engine deltas -> per-choice textual deltas using TextStreamer and packages them into ChatCompletionStreamResponse JSON objects.
    Note: This function reads delta_output fields (group_delta_token_ids, group_finish_reason, request_final_usage_json_str) but does not inspect or merge token-level logprob/top_logprob data. If RequestStreamOutput contained logprobs, this function does not currently extract or include them in the constructed JSON objects.

- TVM_REGISTER_GLOBAL("mlc.json_ffi.CreateJSONFFIEngine").set_body_typed([]() { return Module(make_object<JSONFFIEngineImpl>()); }) — registers factory to create the JSONFFIEngineImpl module for TVM/FFI.

### Relevance to Agent's Goal
The agent is searching for where JSON responses are constructed/merged for logprobs:
- Relevant locations where JSON responses are constructed:
  - StreamBackError — builds an error streaming response and final usage block; serializes via picojson.
  - JSONFFIEngineImpl::GetResponseFromStreamOutput — the central routine that converts engine deltas (Array<RequestStreamOutput>) into serialized JSON response chunks sent to the request_stream_callback_. This is the primary place where per-choice token deltas are transformed into ChatCompletionStreamResponse / ChatCompletionStreamResponseChoice JSON objects.
- Relevant places that propagate logprobs request settings:
  - AddRequest — sets gen_cfg->logprobs = request.logprobs and gen_cfg->top_logprobs = request.top_logprobs, so the downstream engine is informed to produce logprob/top_logprob data.
- Important negative finding: This file does not implement merging or inclusion of token-level logprobs or top_logprobs into the JSON responses. The RequestStreamOutput objects consumed by GetResponseFromStreamOutput (as used here) expose only group_delta_token_ids, group_finish_reason, and request_final_usage_json_str in this code path — no logprob fields are read or serialized here. Therefore, token-level logprob/top_logprobs handling must occur elsewhere (e.g., the threaded engine producing RequestStreamOutput, the RequestStreamOutput structure type definition, or inside TextStreamer and its Put/Finish implementations). To locate merging/inclusion of token-level logprobs and top_logprobs you should inspect:
  - The definition of RequestStreamOutput and its fields,
  - The implementation of serve::ThreadedEngine and how it populates RequestStreamOutput,
  - TextStreamer (methods Put and Finish) for any logprob/top_logprob collection and serialization,
  - Any other code that converts engine-internal logprob data into fields on RequestStreamOutput.

Importance: Hooks engine-produced RequestStreamOutput deltas into the JSON response builder in this file.

    - Iterates delta_outputs.

### File Overview
This file implements JSON serialization and deserialization for the OpenAI-like chat/completion protocol types used by the MLC LLM JSON FFI layer. It converts between picojson::object representations and the C++ domain types (ChatFunction, ChatTool, ChatCompletionMessage, ChatCompletionRequest, response/choice/stream types).

### Architecture
- The file groups functions by type: each domain type typically has a static FromJSON(...) factory that parses a picojson::object (or string) into the C++ structure, and an AsJSON() instance method that converts the structure back into a picojson::object for output.
- Parsing utilities are used via json::Lookup*/LookupOptional* helpers (from "../support/json_parser.h") and Result<T> to propagate errors.
- Top-level response types (ChatCompletionResponse, ChatCompletionStreamResponse) build up JSON objects by calling AsJSON() on contained choices/messages/deltas and aggregating fields like id, created, model, system_fingerprint, object, and optional usage.

The components interoperate: request parsing constructs typed request objects for the model; response AsJSON methods produce the JSON payloads returned to the user. The AsJSON methods are where completion outputs are serialized into JSON.

### Functions & Classes
- ChatFunction::FromJSON — parses a picojson::object into a ChatFunction. It reads "description" (optional), "name" (required), and "parameters" (object -> map<string,string>). Important for parsing declared functions in a tool/function interface.

- ChatFunction::AsJSON — converts a ChatFunction instance to picojson::object. Adds "description" (if present), "name", and "parameters" (object). Used when tools/functions need to be emitted as JSON.

- ChatTool::FromJSON — parses a picojson::object into a ChatTool. It requires a "function" object and delegates to ChatFunction::FromJSON. Important for reading tool definitions.

- ChatTool::AsJSON — converts ChatTool to JSON. Emits "type"="function" and the nested "function" object via ChatFunction::AsJSON.

- ChatFunctionCall::FromJSON — parses a picojson::object into a ChatFunctionCall. Reads required "name" and optional "arguments" (object -> unordered_map<string,string>). Used to parse function call invocation requests.

- ChatFunctionCall::AsJSON — converts ChatFunctionCall to picojson::object, emitting "name" and (if present) "arguments" as an object. Used when returning function call metadata.

- ChatToolCall::FromJSON — parses a picojson::object into a ChatToolCall. Reads a required nested "function" (delegates to ChatFunctionCall::FromJSON) and an optional "id" (overwrites default id if present). Important for parsing tool call entries; note: the implementation contains suspicious code retrieving the optional id (it calls id_res.UnwrapErr() when extracting value), but this is a detail of parsing correctness rather than serialization location.

- ChatToolCall::AsJSON — converts ChatToolCall to JSON. Emits "id", nested "function" (via ChatFunctionCall::AsJSON), and "type"="function".

- ChatCompletionMessage::FromJSON — parses a picojson::object into a ChatCompletionMessage. Handles:
  - "content" that can be a string, null, or array-of-objects (converted into vector<unordered_map<string,string>>).
  - required "role" (validated to be one of "system","user","assistant","tool").
  - optional "name", optional "tool_calls" array (parsed using ChatToolCall::FromJSON), and optional "tool_call_id".
  This is important for parsing individual messages in chat-based requests and responses.

- ChatCompletionRequest::FromJSON — parses a JSON string into ChatCompletionRequest. Reads:
  - required "messages" array (each parsed by ChatCompletionMessage::FromJSON),
  - optional "model", "temperature", "top_p", "max_tokens", "n" (default 1), "frequency_penalty", "presence_penalty", "seed",
  - optional "stop" array -> vector<string>,
  - "tool_choice" (default "auto"),
  - optional "tools" array (each parsed by ChatTool::FromJSON),
  - optional "response_format" parsed by ResponseFormat::FromJSON if present,
  - optional "debug_config" parsed by DebugConfig::FromJSON if present.
  This builds the typed request used by the model. (Note: there is a minor copy/paste error in the code that checks tool_choice_res again when handling tools, but that's peripheral.)

- ChatCompletionMessage::AsJSON — serializes ChatCompletionMessage to picojson::object:
  - serializes content either as "content" string or as "content" array-of-objects (iterates this->content.Parts()).
  - always emits "role".
  - emits optional "name", "tool_call_id", and optional "tool_calls" (array built from ChatToolCall::AsJSON).
  This method is a primary place where message-level JSON is assembled for output.

- ChatCompletionResponseChoice::AsJSON — serializes ChatCompletionResponseChoice to picojson::object:
  - emits "finish_reason": null if not present, otherwise one of the strings "stop","length","tool_calls","error" depending on FinishReason enum value.
  - emits "index" as int64_t cast,
  - emits "message" via this->message.AsJSON().
  This constructs a single choice JSON entry for non-streaming chat completions. This is one of the natural insertion points for adding completion-level fields such as "logprobs"/"top_logprobs" for completed generation results.

- ChatCompletionStreamResponseChoice::AsJSON — serializes ChatCompletionStreamResponseChoice to picojson::object:
  - emits "finish_reason" analogous to ChatCompletionResponseChoice but uses the optional value(),
  - emits "index",
  - emits "delta" via this->delta.AsJSON().
  This constructs a single choice JSON entry for streaming chat completions (delta updates). This is the natural insertion point for attaching incremental/logprob streaming information (token-level logprobs/top_logprobs) into streamed deltas.

- ChatCompletionResponse::AsJSON — serializes ChatCompletionResponse to picojson::object:
  - emits "id", "choices" (array mapped from choices.AsJSON()), "created", "model", "system_fingerprint", and "object".
  This produces the full non-streaming completion response JSON; any choice-level "logprobs" fields would be embedded here through ChatCompletionResponseChoice::AsJSON.

- ChatCompletionStreamResponse::AsJSON — serializes ChatCompletionStreamResponse to picojson::object:
  - emits "id", "choices" (array of stream choices), "created", "model", "system_fingerprint", "object", and optional "usage".
  This produces the streaming response envelope where per-choice deltas (delta.AsJSON()) may include logprob info for streaming responses.

### Relevance to Agent's Goal
Agent goal: "Look for functions that serialize logprobs structures into JSON, merging logic for completion logprobs/top_logprobs" and expects to find builders for CompletionLogProbs, LogProbs, top_logprobs shapes and merging code.

- Direct findings: This file contains the AsJSON serialization methods for messages, response choices, and responses: ChatCompletionMessage::AsJSON, ChatCompletionResponseChoice::AsJSON, ChatCompletionStreamResponseChoice::AsJSON, ChatCompletionResponse::AsJSON, and ChatCompletionStreamResponse::AsJSON. These are the exact locations where any "logprobs", "top_logprobs", or merged token-level logprob arrays would need to be implemented and output into the final JSON.
- Not found: There are no functions, classes, or builders named CompletionLogProbs, LogProbs, top_logprobs, nor any existing merging logic that converts token-level logprob structures into JSON in this file. The code does not currently serialize any logprobs tokens/top_logprobs fields.
- Recommended insertion points: 
  - For non-streaming completions: extend ChatCompletionResponseChoice::AsJSON to add a "logprobs" field (or appropriate shape) into the returned picojson::object, using data available in the choice (e.g., this->message or a dedicated logprobs member if added to the C++ struct).
  - For streaming completions: extend ChatCompletionStreamResponseChoice::AsJSON (or the delta.AsJSON() implementation referenced there) to serialize per-token logprob information incrementally (top_logprobs structures) as part of the "delta" object emitted in streaming responses.
  - For message-level token annotations: ChatCompletionMessage::AsJSON is where per-message fields would be placed if the design stores token-level logprobs inside message/delta.

In summary: this file is responsible for turning domain objects into JSON and is the correct place to add JSON serialization for CompletionLogProbs / LogProbs / top_logprobs, but currently it contains no such serialization or merging logic — you will need to add fields and merging code into the AsJSON methods (most likely ChatCompletionResponseChoice::AsJSON and ChatCompletionStreamResponseChoice::AsJSON, and possibly ChatCompletionMessage::AsJSON or the delta.AsJSON() implementation referenced here).

### File Overview
This header defines the OpenAI-like JSON API protocol structures used by the mlc::llm::json_ffi layer: request/response types, tool/function call types, helper enums, and JSON (de)serialization entrypoints. It is intended to model chat completion requests and both batched and streaming responses.

### Architecture
- Top-level namespace: mlc::llm::json_ffi. 
- Enums: `Type` and `FinishReason` describe content/tool types and why a completion ended.
- Utility: `GenerateUUID` used to create ids (e.g., for `ChatToolCall`).
- Tool/function models: `ChatFunction`, `ChatTool`, `ChatFunctionCall`, `ChatToolCall` hold function/tool metadata + JSON (de)serialization methods.
- Message models: `ChatCompletionMessageContent` (holds either text or "parts") and `ChatCompletionMessage` (role, optional name, tool call metadata).
- Request model: `ChatCompletionRequest` (messages + generation parameters including `logprobs` and `top_logprobs`).
- Response models: `ChatCompletionResponseChoice`, `ChatCompletionStreamResponseChoice`, `ChatCompletionResponse`, `ChatCompletionStreamResponse` with AsJSON() methods for serialization.
- Each class that is expected to be parsed from or emitted to JSON exposes static `FromJSON` and/or `AsJSON` methods to convert between picojson objects and the C++ structures.

The classes are organized so requests carry generation parameters and messages, while responses contain choices (complete and streaming variants) and the top-level response metadata (id, model, created timestamp, system_fingerprint, object type). Many classes have TODOs where additional fields (e.g., logprobs, usage_info) are expected to be added.

### Functions & Classes
- `GenerateUUID` — utility function that returns a random string of specified length using a small charset. Used to generate unique ids (e.g., the default `id` in `ChatToolCall`).

- `Type` (enum class) — enumerates content/tool types: `text`, `json_object`, `function`. Used by `ChatTool` and `ChatToolCall` to indicate the type.

- `FinishReason` (enum class) — enumerates completion finish reasons: `stop`, `length`, `tool_calls`, `error`. Used in response choice types to indicate why generation stopped.

- `ChatFunction` (class)
  - Fields:
    - `std::optional<std::string> description`
    - `std::string name`
    - `std::unordered_map<std::string, std::string> parameters`
  - Methods:
    - `static Result<ChatFunction> FromJSON(const picojson::object& json)` — parse a JSON object into a `ChatFunction`.
    - `picojson::object AsJSON() const` — serialize `ChatFunction` to a picojson object.
  - Importance: Represents a function declaration for tool calls; needed when request or tools include functions.

- `ChatTool` (class)
  - Fields:
    - `Type type` (default `Type::function`)
    - `ChatFunction function`
  - Methods:
    - `static Result<ChatTool> FromJSON(const picojson::object& json)`
    - `picojson::object AsJSON() const`
  - Importance: Wrapper modeling a tool: primarily used in `ChatCompletionRequest::tools`.

- `ChatFunctionCall` (class)
  - Fields:
    - `std::string name`
    - `std::optional<std::unordered_map<std::string, std::string>> arguments`
  - Methods:
    - `static Result<ChatFunctionCall> FromJSON(const picojson::object& json)`
    - `picojson::object AsJSON() const`
  - Importance: Represents an invocation of a function (tool) and its arguments.

- `ChatToolCall` (class)
  - Fields:
    - `std::string id = "call_" + GenerateUUID(8)`
    - `Type type = Type::function`
    - `ChatFunctionCall function`
  - Methods:
    - `static Result<ChatToolCall> FromJSON(const picojson::object& json)`
    - `picojson::object AsJSON() const`
  - Importance: Concrete tool call instance with an id; used in `ChatCompletionMessage::tool_calls` and tooling flows.

- `ChatCompletionMessageContent` (class)
  - Constructors:
    - `ChatCompletionMessageContent()` (default)
    - `ChatCompletionMessageContent(std::nullopt_t)` — allows explicit null content
    - `ChatCompletionMessageContent(std::string text)` — set text variant
    - `ChatCompletionMessageContent(std::vector<std::unordered_map<std::string, std::string>> parts)` — set parts variant
  - Methods:
    - `bool IsNull() const`
    - `bool IsText() const`
    - `bool IsParts() const`
    - `const std::string& Text() const`
    - `const std::vector<std::unordered_map<std::string, std::string>>& Parts() const`
  - Fields:
    - `std::optional<std::string> text_`
    - `std::optional<std::vector<std::unordered_map<std::string, std::string>>> parts_`
  - Importance: Encapsulates message content which can be either plain `text` or a `parts` structure. If token-level logprobs are to be associated with the raw text, this class is a candidate to attach such per-message/token info.

- `ChatCompletionMessage` (class)
  - Fields:
    - `ChatCompletionMessageContent content = std::nullopt`
    - `std::string role`
    - `std::optional<std::string> name`
    - `std::optional<std::vector<ChatToolCall>> tool_calls`
    - `std::optional<std::string> tool_call_id`
  - Methods:
    - `static Result<ChatCompletionMessage> FromJSON(const picojson::object& json)`
    - `picojson::object AsJSON() const`
  - Importance: Models the message (role + content) used in requests and responses. If you want to attach logprob metadata at the message level, adding fields here is an option.

- `ChatCompletionRequest` (class)

### File Overview
This header defines the in-memory data structures that track per-request and per-generation state during text generation in the serving engine (RequestModelState, RequestStateEntry, RequestState, and related helpers). It contains fields and methods that manage generated tokens, draft/speculative tokens, and the post-processing workspace used to produce streamed deltas (including structures that carry token ids and logprob JSON strings).

### Architecture
- RequestModelStateNode holds the state of a single user request with respect to a single model (committed tokens, draft/speculative tokens, input/prefill bookkeeping, and optional grammar matcher).
- RequestStateEntryNode represents one generation branch / prefix of a user request; it aggregates per-model RequestModelState instances (mstates), RNG, stop handlers, and the logic to produce delta outputs for streaming.
- RequestStateNode groups all RequestStateEntry instances for a request, plus request-level metrics and the post-processing workspace.
- RequestActionPostProcWorkspace contains reusable per-request buffers (stream_outputs) used during action post-processing to avoid repeated allocations; its GetStreamOutput provides a reusable RequestStreamOutput (declared elsewhere).
- DeltaRequestReturn is a POD used to represent the delta output for API returns (delta token ids, JSON logprob strings per delta token, optional finish reason and extra_prefix_string).
- The header exposes methods that do token commit/rollback, manage draft tokens, compute/return next-token bitmasks for grammar constraints, and convert internal token/logprob state to streaming outputs (notably RequestStateEntryNode::GetDeltaRequestReturn).

Components interact as:
- The serving engine updates RequestModelStateNode instances during decoding (CommitToken, AddDraftToken, RemoveAllDraftTokens, RollbackTokens).
- When producing output to clients, the engine uses RequestStateEntryNode::GetDeltaRequestReturn which uses Tokenizer to prepare logprob information and writes into RequestStreamOutput buffers from RequestActionPostProcWorkspace.

### Functions & Classes

- RequestModelStateNode — Object
  - Fields:
    - Request request — the logical request this model-state belongs to.
    - int64_t internal_id — physical index in running request queue (-1 if on hold).
    - int model_id — the model index corresponding to this state.
    - std::vector<SampleResult> committed_tokens — committed generated tokens and related probability info. (Highly relevant: this is the primary place committed token ids and their per-token probability/logprob info would be stored. SampleResult struct (defined elsewhere) likely contains token id and logprob/top_logprobs.)
    - Array<Data> inputs — input data still to prefill for this model.
    - std::vector<Data> prefilled_inputs — inputs already prefilled for prefix cache notification.
    - int64_t cached_committed_tokens — number of tokens already cached in prefix cache.
    - int64_t num_prefilled_tokens — number of tokens already prefilled from inputs.
    - int num_tokens_for_next_decode — tokens to process in next decode.
    - bool require_retokenization_in_next_decode — retokenization flag for jump-forward decoding.
    - std::vector<SampleResult> draft_output_tokens — draft/generated tokens from small speculative models. (Relevant: draft tokens also carry probability info similarly to committed_tokens.)
    - std::vector<int> draft_token_slots — storage slot indices for draft tokens.
    - std::vector<int64_t> draft_token_parent_idx — parent indices of draft tokens.
    - std::vector<int64_t> draft_token_first_child_idx — first child indices of draft tokens.
    - std::unordered_map<int32_t,int32_t> appeared_token_ids — counts / occurrence times for tokens that appeared among committed/draft tokens.
    - std::optional<xgrammar::GrammarMatcher> grammar_matcher — optional grammar matching state for grammar-guided generation.
  - Methods:
    - int GetInputLength() const — returns total length of input data.
    - bool RequireNextTokenBitmask() — returns whether next-token bitmask is needed (grammar-guided).
    - void GetNextTokenBitmask(DLTensor* bitmask) — fills provided DLTensor with next-token bitmask (uint32_t array); used for grammar masking.
    - void CommitToken(SampleResult sampled_token) — commit a new token into committed_tokens; updates appeared_token_ids and grammar state but does not affect KV cache.
    - void RollbackTokens(int count) — rollback last 'count' tokens from committed_tokens; also rollbacks appeared_token_ids and grammar state.
    - void AddDraftToken(SampleResult sampled_token, int draft_token_slot, int64_t parent_idx) — add a draft token to draft_output_tokens and update appeared_token_ids.
    - void RemoveAllDraftTokens(std::vector<int>* removed_draft_token_slots = nullptr) — remove all draft tokens and update appeared_token_ids; optionally return removed slots.
  - Importance: RequestModelStateNode is the core place where token-level outputs and their probability metadata (SampleResult entries) are stored per model. Any token-level logprob data will be found in committed_tokens and draft_output_tokens or accessible via methods that read them.

- RequestModelState — ObjectRef wrapper
  - explicit RequestModelState(Request request, int model_id, int64_t internal_id, Array<Data> inputs, const std::optional<xgrammar::CompiledGrammar>& compiled_grammar)
  - Importance: constructor used to create per-model state objects.

- DeltaRequestReturn — struct
  - std::vector<int64_t> delta_token_ids — token ids to be returned in this delta.
  - std::vector<String> delta_logprob_json_strs — logprob JSON strings for each delta token id. (Highly relevant: this field explicitly carries token-level logprob information as JSON strings.)
  - Optional<String> finish_reason — optional finish reason to return.
  - String extra_prefix_string — extra string to prepend before detokenized delta (default "").
  - Importance: defines the canonical shape for deltas that include both token ids and token-level logprob JSON strings to be returned to the caller.

- RequestActionPostProcWorkspace — struct
  - std::vector<RequestStreamOutput> stream_outputs — reusable per-request buffers for streaming outputs. (RequestStreamOutput type is declared elsewhere.)
  - RequestStreamOutput GetStreamOutput() — returns a RequestStreamOutput from stream_outputs (reuses buffers to avoid allocation).
  - Importance: stream_outputs holds RequestStreamOutput objects used during postprocessing; GetStreamOutput is the allocator/recycler for per-entry streaming output buffers. These buffers are where token ids and logprob strings are likely assembled for streaming.

- RequestStateStatus — enum class
  - kPending, kAlive, kFinished — statuses for RequestStateEntryNode.

- RequestStateEntryNode — Object
  - Fields:
    - RequestStateStatus status — status of this entry.
    - Request request — the request this entry is for.
    - int parent_idx — index of parent entry (-1 if none).
    - std::vector<int> child_indices — children indices in RequestState.
    - Array<RequestModelState> mstates — state of this entry with respect to each model. (Critical: token/logprob storage lives inside each RequestModelStateNode in mstates.)
    - RandomGenerator rng — RNG for decoding decisions.
    - StopStrHandler stop_str_handler — stop string handling for termination.
    - int next_callback_token_pos — start index of committed tokens to include in next stream callback.
    - std::string extra_prefix_string — extra string to prepend in outputs for this entry.
    - std::vector<int32_t> token_ids_for_prefix_cache_update — token ids used for prefix cache update.
    - RequestStateNode* rstate — back-reference to parent RequestStateNode.
  - Methods:
    - void GetDeltaRequestReturn(const Tokenizer& tokenizer, int64_t max_single_sequence_length, RequestStreamOutput* delta_stream_output, int idx)

### File Overview
This header defines the in-memory data structures that track per-request and per-generation state during text generation in the serving engine (RequestModelState, RequestStateEntry, RequestState, and related helpers). It contains fields and methods that manage generated tokens, draft/speculative tokens, and the post-processing workspace used to produce streamed deltas (including structures that carry token ids and logprob JSON strings).

### Architecture
- RequestModelStateNode holds the state of a single user request with respect to a single model (committed tokens, draft/speculative tokens, input/prefill bookkeeping, and optional grammar matcher).
- RequestStateEntryNode represents one generation branch / prefix of a user request; it aggregates per-model RequestModelState instances (mstates), RNG, stop handlers, and the logic to produce delta outputs for streaming.
- RequestStateNode groups all RequestStateEntry instances for a request, plus request-level metrics and the post-processing workspace.
- RequestActionPostProcWorkspace contains reusable per-request buffers (stream_outputs) used during action post-processing to avoid repeated allocations; its GetStreamOutput provides a reusable RequestStreamOutput (declared elsewhere).
- DeltaRequestReturn is a POD used to represent the delta output for API returns (delta token ids, JSON logprob strings per delta token, optional finish reason and extra_prefix_string).
- The header exposes methods that do token commit/rollback, manage draft tokens, compute/return next-token bitmasks for grammar constraints, and convert internal token/logprob state to streaming outputs (notably RequestStateEntryNode::GetDeltaRequestReturn).

Components interact as:
- The serving engine updates RequestModelStateNode instances during decoding (CommitToken, AddDraftToken, RemoveAllDraftTokens, RollbackTokens).
- When producing output to clients, the engine uses RequestStateEntryNode::GetDeltaRequestReturn which uses Tokenizer to prepare logprob information and writes into RequestStreamOutput buffers from RequestActionPostProcWorkspace.

  - Fields:

    - int model_id — the model index corresponding to this state.

(Highly relevant: this is the primary place committed token ids and their per-token probability/logprob info would be stored. SampleResult struct (defined elsewhere) likely contains token id and logprob/top_logprobs.)
    - Array<Data> inputs — input data still to prefill for this model.
    - std::vector<Data> prefilled_inputs — inputs already prefilled for prefix cache notification.
    - int64_t cached_committed_tokens — number of tokens already cached in prefix cache.
    - int64_t num_prefilled_tokens — number of tokens already prefilled from inputs.
    - int num_tokens_for_next_decode — tokens to process in next decode.
    - bool require_retokenization_in_next_decode — retokenization flag for jump-forward decoding.
    - std::vector<SampleResult> draft_output_tokens — draft/generated tokens from small speculative models. (Relevant: draft tokens also carry probability info similarly to committed_tokens.)
    - std::vector<int> draft_token_slots — storage slot indices for draft tokens.
    - std::vector<int64_t> draft_token_parent_idx — parent indices of draft tokens.
    - std::vector<int64_t> draft_token_first_child_idx — first child indices of draft tokens.
    - std::unordered_map<int32_t,int32_t> appeared_token_ids — counts / occurrence times for tokens that appeared among committed/draft tokens.
    - std::optional<xgrammar::GrammarMatcher> grammar_matcher — optional grammar matching state for grammar-guided generation.
  - Methods:
    - int GetInputLength() const — returns total length of input data.
    - bool RequireNextTokenBitmask() — returns whether next-token bitmask is needed (grammar-guided).
    - void GetNextTokenBitmask(DLTensor* bitmask) — fills provided DLTensor with next-token bitmask (uint32_t array); used for grammar masking.
    - void CommitToken(SampleResult sampled_token) — commit a new token into committed_tokens; updates appeared_token_ids and grammar state but does not affect KV cache.
    - void RollbackTokens(int count) — rollback last 'count' tokens from committed_tokens; also rollbacks appeared_token_ids and grammar state.
    - void AddDraftToken(SampleResult sampled_token, int draft_token_slot, int64_t parent_idx) — add a draft token to draft_output_tokens and update appeared_token_ids.
    - void RemoveAllDraftTokens(std::vector<int>* removed_draft_token_slots = nullptr) — remove all draft tokens and update appeared_token_ids; optionally return removed slots.

tool_calls.result:
### File Overview
This file implements request-level state objects and post-processing for streamed generation results: RequestModelState (per-model state), RequestStateEntry (per-request entry with per-model states), RequestActionPostProcWorkspace stream output handling, and the key method RequestStateEntryNode::GetDeltaRequestReturn which constructs per-request delta outputs including group_delta_token_ids and group_delta_logprob_json_strs.

### Architecture
- The file uses TVM object wrappers: RequestModelState wraps RequestModelStateNode, RequestStateEntry wraps RequestStateEntryNode, and RequestState wraps RequestStateNode. These node types are registered with TVM via TVM_REGISTER_OBJECT_TYPE.
- RequestModelStateNode holds per-model runtime state (inputs, committed tokens, draft tokens, a grammar matcher, counters).
- RequestStateEntryNode holds a request-level entry composed of an array of RequestModelState (mstates), a StopStrHandler, RNG, next_callback_token_pos, extra_prefix_string, and postproc output bookkeeping.
- RequestActionPostProcWorkspace manages a list of RequestStreamOutput objects (stream_outputs) and provides GetStreamOutput() to obtain an "unpacked" stream output slot, creating one if necessary.
- RequestStateNode aggregates multiple RequestStateEntry objects and prepares an initial RequestStreamOutput (with optional group_delta_logprob_json_strs) and marks it unpacked for the postprocessor.

Interaction flow:
- During generation, RequestModelStateNode::CommitToken records SampleResult tokens, and RequestStateEntryNode::GetDeltaRequestReturn reads committed tokens and fills a RequestStreamOutput (delta_stream_output) per response index, including pushing JSON logprob strings (if requested) and setting finish reasons.
- RequestActionPostProcWorkspace::GetStreamOutput and the constructor RequestState::RequestState prepare and return RequestStreamOutput objects that contain group_delta_token_ids and an optional group_delta_logprob_json_strs.

### Functions & Classes

- TVM_REGISTER_OBJECT_TYPE(RequestModelStateNode)
  - Registers the RequestModelStateNode type with the TVM object system. Important for object creation and serialization via TVM.

- RequestModelState::RequestModelState
  - Constructor taking (Request request, int model_id, int64_t internal_id, Array<Data> inputs, const std::optional<xgrammar::CompiledGrammar>& compiled_grammar).
  - Creates and initializes a RequestModelStateNode, setting model_id, internal_id, inputs, grammar_matcher (if compiled_grammar provided) and stores the request. Essential for per-model initialization including optional grammar matching state.

- RequestModelStateNode::GetInputLength
  - Computes total input length by summing GetLength() over inputs.
  - Used to compute prompt/input lengths where needed (e.g., enforcement of max sequence length).

- RequestModelStateNode::RequireNextTokenBitmask
  - Returns whether grammar matching is active (grammar_matcher.has_value()).
  - Indicates whether the next-token bitmask should be requested from the model (restricting next tokens).

- RequestModelStateNode::GetNextTokenBitmask
  - Populates a provided DLTensor* bitmask using grammar_matcher->GetNextTokenBitmask.
  - Used to enforce grammar constraints at sampling time.

- RequestModelStateNode::CommitToken
  - Signature: void CommitToken(SampleResult sampled_token)
  - Appends sampled_token to committed_tokens, increments appeared_token_ids counter, increments num_tokens_for_next_decode, and calls grammar_matcher->AcceptToken(token) if grammar matcher exists (ICHECK accepted).
  - Central point for making a token final/committed in the state. This affects what GetDeltaRequestReturn will later send to the client and updates grammar matcher state.

- RequestModelStateNode::RollbackTokens
  - Signature: void RollbackTokens(int count)
  - Removes `count` tokens from committed_tokens, updates appeared_token_ids, and calls grammar_matcher->Rollback(1) per token if grammar matcher exists.
  - Used to revert committed decisions (e.g., beam pruning or other backtracking).

- RequestModelStateNode::AddDraftToken
  - Signature: void AddDraftToken(SampleResult sampled_token, int draft_token_slot, int64_t parent_idx)
  - Stores draft (non-committed) sampled tokens and bookkeeping arrays (draft_token_slots, parent/child index tracking).
  - Used by decoders that explore multiple hypothesis (draft tokens), separate from committed tokens.

- RequestModelStateNode::RemoveAllDraftTokens
  - Signature: void RemoveAllDraftTokens(std::vector<int>* removed_draft_token_slots)
  - Clears all draft token state and optionally returns unique draft_token_slots that were removed.
  - Important for cleaning up exploration artifacts when they are no longer needed.

- RequestActionPostProcWorkspace::GetStreamOutput
  - Signature: RequestStreamOutput GetStreamOutput()
  - Returns the first "unpacked" RequestStreamOutput from stream_outputs; if none exist, creates a new RequestStreamOutput based on stream_outputs[0] structure: it copies the request_id, initializes group_delta_token_ids and group_extra_prefix_string, and conditionally creates group_delta_logprob_json_strs if present in the base stream_output. The new stream_output is appended to stream_outputs and returned.
  - Important for producing an output slot that the post-processor can fill; this function also dictates how group_delta_logprob_json_strs (optional) are created/propagated for additional output instances.

- TVM_REGISTER_OBJECT_TYPE(RequestStateEntryNode)
  - Registers the RequestStateEntryNode type with TVM.

- RequestStateEntry::RequestStateEntry
  - Constructor taking (Request request, int num_models, int64_t internal_id, int rng_seed, const std::vector<std::string>& token_table, const std::optional<xgrammar::CompiledGrammar>& compiled_grammar, int parent_idx)
  - Builds a RequestStateEntryNode: creates an Array<RequestModelState> mstates sized num_models (each constructed with the provided inputs or parent inputs), initializes status, RandomGenerator rng, StopStrHandler stop_str_handler (configured with generation_cfg debug ignore_eos handling and token_table), sets request, parent_idx, and next_callback_token_pos.
  - Sets up per-request state including stop handling and per-model mstates. Next_callback_token_pos used to track how many committed tokens already delivered to callbacks/deltas.

- RequestStateEntryNode::GetDeltaRequestReturn
  - Signature:
    void GetDeltaRequestReturn(const Tokenizer& tokenizer, int64_t max_single_sequence_length, RequestStreamOutput* delta_stream_output, int idx)
  - This is the key method that constructs the per-response "delta" output for streaming (the agent's primary area of interest). Behavior:
    - Validates delta_stream_output and whether logprobs are required (needs_logprobs).
    - Clears (*delta_stream_output)->group_delta_token_ids[idx] and, if present, group_delta_logprob_json_strs.value()[idx], sets group_finish_reason[idx] to NullOpt, sets group_extra_prefix_string[idx] from this->extra_prefix_string then clears extra_prefix_string.
    - Iterates over committed_tokens starting from next_callback_token_pos:
      - Adds token ids to (*delta_stream_output)->group_delta_token_ids[idx] via stop_str_handler->Put(...).
      - If needs_logprobs, calls committed_tokens[pos].GetLogProbJSON(tokenizer, request->generation_cfg->logprobs) and push_back the returned JSON string into (*delta_stream_output)->group_delta_logprob_json_strs.value()[idx].
      - Increments next_callback_token_pos.
      - If stop_str_handler->StopTriggered(), sets group_finish_reason[idx] = "stop" and breaks.
    - After collecting tokens, performs stop-token handling: if not ignoring EOS, scans group_delta_token_ids[idx] and if any token equals one of request->generation_cfg->stop_token_ids then sets group_finish_reason[idx] = "stop" and truncates group_delta_token_ids[idx] to remove the matched stop token and all tokens after it.
    - If grammar matcher exists and has been terminated and group_finish_reason != "stop": pops the last token from group_delta_token_ids[idx], sets group_finish_reason[idx] = "stop". (This handles the case where a grammar termination token was used just to trigger termination and should not be returned.)
    - If group_finish_reason is set, returns early.
    - Enforces generation limits:
      - If request->generation_cfg->max_tokens >= 0 and total num_committed_tokens >= max_tokens: uses stop_str_handler->Finish to append any finishing tokens, sets group_finish_reason[idx] = "length", and returns.
      - If request->prompt_tokens + num_committed_tokens >= max_single_sequence_length: uses stop_str_handler->Finish and sets group_finish_reason[idx] = "length".
  - Importance:
    - This method is the implementation point where committed SampleResult tokens are transformed into the RequestStreamOutput fields:
      - group_delta_token_ids[idx] (the token ids to stream to client)
      - group_delta_logprob_json_strs.value()[idx] (if requires logprobs: the JSON logprob string per token produced by SampleResult::GetLogProbJSON)
      - group_finish_reason[idx] and group_extra_prefix_string[idx]
    - It also implements logic to avoid re-sending tokens (next_callback_token_pos), incorporate stop-string and stop-token semantics (including trimming of stop token), handle grammar termination, and respect generation length limits.
    - This function therefore contains the merging/aggregation logic of tokens and their corresponding logprob JSONs for the delta output.

- TVM_REGISTER_OBJECT_TYPE(RequestStateNode)
  - Registers the RequestStateNode type.

- RequestState::RequestState
  - Constructor taking (std::vector<RequestStateEntry> entries, int num_response, std::chrono::high_resolution_clock::time_point add_time_point)
  - Creates a RequestStateNode, sets metrics.prompt_tokens from the first entry's request, records add_time_point, allocates group_delta_token_ids, optionally allocates group_delta_logprob_json_strs if entries[0]->request->generation_cfg->logprobs is true, and constructs a RequestStreamOutput with these fields. Marks that initial stream_output->unpacked = true and places it in postproc_states.stream_outputs.
  - Importance:
    - This constructs the initial RequestStreamOutput template used by post-processing; it defines whether group_delta_logprob_json_strs will be present in stream outputs (based on generation_cfg->logprobs) and sets up an unpacked stream output to be filled by later processing. This is the origin of the RequestStreamOutput structures that RequestStateEntryNode::GetDeltaRequestReturn will populate.

### Relevance to Agent's Goal
- RequestStateEntryNode::GetDeltaRequestReturn is the primary implementation the agent wanted to inspect: it shows exactly where delta_logprob_json_strs are formed (the push_back of committed_tokens[pos].GetLogProbJSON(tokenizer, request->generation_cfg->logprobs) into (*delta_stream_output)->group_delta_logprob_json_strs.value()[idx]) and how tokens and logprob JSON strings are appended in lockstep while iterating committed_tokens from next_callback_token_pos.
- The merging logic: tokens are collected in order from committed_tokens into (*delta_stream_output)->group_delta_token_ids[idx]; corresponding logprob JSON strings are appended to group_delta_logprob_json_strs.value()[idx] if requested. There is no cross-token aggregation beyond parallel push_back; trimming/merging occurs via stop_str_handler, stop token detection (truncation of the vector at matched stop token index), grammar termination (pop back of last token), and generation-length finishing (stop_str_handler->Finish).
- Conversion to RequestStreamOutput fields:
  - RequestState::RequestState builds the RequestStreamOutput template and decides whether group_delta_logprob_json_strs exists (based on generation_cfg->logprobs).
  - RequestActionPostProcWorkspace::GetStreamOutput shows how additional unpacked RequestStreamOutput entries are created copying the presence/absence of group_delta_logprob_json_strs and initializing per-response vectors for group_delta_token_ids and group_delta_logprob_json_strs.
  - RequestStateEntryNode::GetDeltaRequestReturn writes directly into the RequestStreamOutput fields: group_delta_token_ids[idx], group_delta_logprob_json_strs.value()[idx], group_finish_reason[idx], and group_extra_prefix_string[idx].
- Additional relevant points:
  - next_callback_token_pos ensures tokens are not re-sent; commit/rollback logic in RequestModelStateNode controls what tokens appear in committed_tokens.
  - stop_str_handler is responsible for grouping tokens and detecting stop sequences (it inserts token ids into group_delta_token_ids[idx] via Put and can append finishing tokens via Finish).
  - Grammar matcher presence affects both bitmask requests (RequireNextTokenBitmask/GetNextTokenBitmask) and termination handling in GetDeltaRequestReturn (IsTerminated triggers removal of last token and finish).

If you want, I can point to the exact lines where GetLogProbJSON is called and where group_delta_logprob_json_strs is created/checked, or extract the minimal code paths for logprob formation and token trimming.

### File Overview
This file implements request-level state objects and post-processing for streamed generation results: RequestModelState (per-model state), RequestStateEntry (per-request entry with per-model states), RequestActionPostProcWorkspace stream output handling, and the key method RequestStateEntryNode::GetDeltaRequestReturn which constructs per-request delta outputs including group_delta_token_ids and group_delta_logprob_json_strs.

### Architecture
- The file uses TVM object wrappers: RequestModelState wraps RequestModelStateNode, RequestStateEntry wraps RequestStateEntryNode, and RequestState wraps RequestStateNode. These node types are registered with TVM via TVM_REGISTER_OBJECT_TYPE.
- RequestModelStateNode holds per-model runtime state (inputs, committed tokens, draft tokens, a grammar matcher, counters).
- RequestStateEntryNode holds a request-level entry composed of an array of RequestModelState (mstates), a StopStrHandler, RNG, next_callback_token_pos, extra_prefix_string, and postproc output bookkeeping.
- RequestActionPostProcWorkspace manages a list of RequestStreamOutput objects (stream_outputs) and provides GetStreamOutput() to obtain an "unpacked" stream output slot, creating one if necessary.
- RequestStateNode aggregates multiple RequestStateEntry objects and prepares an initial RequestStreamOutput (with optional group_delta_logprob_json_strs) and marks it unpacked for the postprocessor.

Interaction flow:
- During generation, RequestModelStateNode::CommitToken records SampleResult tokens, and RequestStateEntryNode::GetDeltaRequestReturn reads committed tokens and fills a RequestStreamOutput (delta_stream_output) per response index, including pushing JSON logprob strings (if requested) and setting finish reasons.
- RequestActionPostProcWorkspace::GetStreamOutput and the constructor RequestState::RequestState prepare and return RequestStreamOutput objects that contain group_delta_token_ids and an optional group_delta_logprob_json_strs.

### Functions & Classes

- TVM_REGISTER_OBJECT_TYPE(RequestModelStateNode)
  - Registers the RequestModelStateNode type with the TVM object system. Important for object creation and serialization via TVM.

- RequestModelState::RequestModelState
  - Constructor taking (Request request, int model_id, int64_t internal_id, Array<Data> inputs, const std::optional<xgrammar::CompiledGrammar>& compiled_grammar).
  - Creates and initializes a RequestModelStateNode, setting model_id, internal_id, inputs, grammar_matcher (if compiled_grammar provided) and stores the request. Essential for per-model initialization including optional grammar matching state.

- RequestModelStateNode::GetInputLength
  - Computes total input length by summing GetLength() over inputs.
  - Used to compute prompt/input lengths where needed (e.g., enforcement of max sequence length).

- RequestModelStateNode::RequireNextTokenBitmask
  - Returns whether grammar matching is active (grammar_matcher.has_value()).
  - Indicates whether the next-token bitmask should be requested from the model (restricting next tokens).

- RequestModelStateNode::GetNextTokenBitmask
  - Populates a provided DLTensor* bitmask using grammar_matcher->GetNextTokenBitmask.
  - Used to enforce grammar constraints at sampling time.

- RequestModelStateNode::CommitToken
  - Signature: void CommitToken(SampleResult sampled_token)
  - Appends sampled_token to committed_tokens, increments appeared_token_ids counter, increments num_tokens_for_next_decode, and calls grammar_matcher->AcceptToken(token) if grammar matcher exists (ICHECK accepted).
  - Central point for making a token final/committed in the state. This affects what GetDeltaRequestReturn will later send to the client and updates grammar matcher state.

- RequestModelStateNode::RollbackTokens
  - Signature: void RollbackTokens(int count)
  - Removes `count` tokens from committed_tokens, updates appeared_token_ids, and calls grammar_matcher->Rollback(1) per token if grammar matcher exists.
  - Used to revert committed decisions (e.g., beam pruning or other backtracking).

- RequestModelStateNode::AddDraftToken
  - Signature: void AddDraftToken(SampleResult sampled_token, int draft_token_slot, int64_t parent_idx)
  - Stores draft (non-committed) sampled tokens and bookkeeping arrays (draft_token_slots, parent/child index tracking).
  - Used by decoders that explore multiple hypothesis (draft tokens), separate from committed tokens.

- RequestModelStateNode::RemoveAllDraftTokens
  - Signature: void RemoveAllDraftTokens(std::vector<int>* removed_draft_token_slots)
  - Clears all draft token state and optionally returns unique draft_token_slots that were removed.
  - Important for cleaning up exploration artifacts when they are no longer needed.

- RequestActionPostProcWorkspace::GetStreamOutput
  - Signature: RequestStreamOutput GetStreamOutput()
  - Returns the first "unpacked" RequestStreamOutput from stream_outputs; if none exist, creates a new RequestStreamOutput based on stream_outputs[0] structure: it copies the request_id, initializes group_delta_token_ids and group_extra_prefix_string, and conditionally creates group_delta_logprob_json_strs if present in the base stream_output. The new stream_output is appended to stream_outputs and returned.
  - Important for producing an output slot that the post-processor can fill; this function also dictates how group_delta_logprob_json_strs (optional) are created/propagated for additional output instances.

- TVM_REGISTER_OBJECT_TYPE(RequestStateEntryNode)
  - Registers the RequestStateEntryNode type with TVM.

- RequestStateEntry::RequestStateEntry
  - Constructor taking (Request request, int num_models, int64_t internal_id, int rng_seed, const std::vector<std::string>& token_table, const std::optional<xgrammar::CompiledGrammar>& compiled_grammar, int parent_idx)
  - Builds a RequestStateEntryNode: creates an Array<RequestModelState> mstates sized num_models (each constructed with the provided inputs or parent inputs), initializes status, RandomGenerator rng, StopStrHandler stop_str_handler (configured with generation_cfg debug ignore_eos handling and token_table), sets request, parent_idx, and next_callback_token_pos.
  - Sets up per-request state including stop handling and per-model mstates. Next_callback_token_pos used to track how many committed tokens already delivered to callbacks/deltas.

- RequestStateEntryNode::GetDeltaRequestReturn
  - Signature:
    void GetDeltaRequestReturn(const Tokenizer& tokenizer, int64_t max_single_sequence_length, RequestStreamOutput* delta_stream_output, int idx)
  - This is the key method that constructs the per-response "delta" output for streaming (the agent's primary area of interest). Behavior:
    - Validates delta_stream_output and whether logprobs are required (needs_logprobs).
    - Clears (*delta_stream_output)->group_delta_token_ids[idx] and, if present, group_delta_logprob_json_strs.value()[idx], sets group_finish_reason[idx] to NullOpt, sets group_extra_prefix_string[idx] from this->extra_prefix_string then clears extra_prefix_string.
    - Iterates over committed_tokens starting from next_callback_token_pos:
      - Adds token ids to (*delta_stream_output)->group_delta_token_ids[idx] via stop_str_handler->Put(...).
      - If needs_logprobs, calls committed_tokens[pos].GetLogProbJSON(tokenizer, request->generation_cfg->logprobs) and push_back the returned JSON string into (*delta_stream_output)->group_delta_logprob_json_strs.value()[idx].
      - Increments next_callback_token_pos.
      - If stop_str_handler->StopTriggered(), sets group_finish_reason[idx] = "stop" and breaks.
    - After collecting tokens, performs stop-token handling: if not ignoring EOS, scans group_delta_token_ids[idx] and if any token equals one of request->generation_cfg->stop_token_ids then sets group_finish_reason[idx] = "stop" and truncates group_delta_token_ids[idx] to remove the matched stop token and all tokens after it.
    - If grammar matcher exists and has been terminated and group_finish_reason != "stop": pops the last token from group_delta_token_ids[idx], sets group_finish_reason[idx] = "stop". (This handles the case where a grammar termination token was used just to trigger termination and should not be returned.)
    - If group_finish_reason is set, returns early.
    - Enforces generation limits:
      - If request->generation_cfg->max_tokens >= 0 and total num_committed_tokens >= max_tokens: uses stop_str_handler->Finish to append any finishing tokens, sets group_finish_reason[idx] = "length", and returns.
      - If request->prompt_tokens + num_committed_tokens >= max_single_sequence_length: uses stop_str_handler->Finish and sets group_finish_reason[idx] = "length".
  - Importance:
    - This method is the implementation point where committed SampleResult tokens are transformed into the RequestStreamOutput fields:
      - group_delta_token_ids[idx] (the token ids to stream to client)
      - group_delta_logprob_json_strs.value()[idx] (if requires logprobs: the JSON logprob string per token produced by SampleResult::GetLogProbJSON)
      - group_finish_reason[idx] and group_extra_prefix_string[idx]
    - It also implements logic to avoid re-sending tokens (next_callback_token_pos), incorporate stop-string and stop-token semantics (including trimming of stop token), handle grammar termination, and respect generation length limits.
    - This function therefore contains the merging/aggregation logic of tokens and their corresponding logprob JSONs for the delta output.

- TVM_REGISTER_OBJECT_TYPE(RequestStateNode)
  - Registers the RequestStateNode type.

- RequestState::RequestState
  - Constructor taking (std::vector<RequestStateEntry> entries, int num_response, std::chrono::high_resolution_clock::time_point add_time_point)
  - Creates a RequestStateNode, sets metrics.prompt_tokens from the first entry's request, records add_time_point, allocates group_delta_token_ids, optionally allocates group_delta_logprob_json_strs if entries[0]->request->generation_cfg->logprobs is true, and constructs a RequestStreamOutput with these fields. Marks that initial stream_output->unpacked = true and places it in postproc_states.stream_outputs.
  - Importance:
    - This constructs the initial RequestStreamOutput template used by post-processing; it defines whether group_delta_logprob_json_strs will be present in stream outputs (based on generation_cfg->logprobs) and sets up an unpacked stream output to be filled by later processing. This is the origin of the RequestStreamOutput structures that RequestStateEntryNode::GetDeltaRequestReturn will populate.

### Relevance to Agent's Goal
- RequestStateEntryNode::GetDeltaRequestReturn is the primary implementation the agent wanted to inspect: it shows exactly where delta_logprob_json_strs are formed (the push_back of committed_tokens[pos].GetLogProbJSON(tokenizer, request->generation_cfg->logprobs) into (*delta_stream_output)->group_delta_logprob_json_strs.value()[idx]) and how tokens and logprob JSON strings are appended in lockstep while iterating committed_tokens from next_callback_token_pos.
- The merging logic: tokens are collected in order from committed_tokens into (*delta_stream_output)->group_delta_token_ids[idx]; corresponding logprob JSON strings are appended to group_delta_logprob_json_strs.value()[idx] if requested. There is no cross-token aggregation beyond parallel push_back; trimming/merging occurs via stop_str_handler, stop token detection (truncation of the vector at matched stop token index), grammar termination (pop back of last token), and generation-length finishing (stop_str_handler->Finish).
- Conversion to RequestStreamOutput fields:
  - RequestState::RequestState builds the RequestStreamOutput template and decides whether group_delta_logprob_json_strs exists (based on generation_cfg->logprobs).
  - RequestActionPostProcWorkspace::GetStreamOutput shows how additional unpacked RequestStreamOutput entries are created copying the presence/absence of group_delta_logprob_json_strs and initializing per-response vectors for group_delta_token_ids and group_delta_logprob_json_strs.
  - RequestStateEntryNode::GetDeltaRequestReturn writes directly into the RequestStreamOutput fields: group_delta_token_ids[idx], group_delta_logprob_json_strs.value()[idx], group_finish_reason[idx], and group_extra_prefix_string[idx].
- Additional relevant points:
  - next_callback_token_pos ensures tokens are not re-sent; commit/rollback logic in RequestModelStateNode controls what tokens appear in committed_tokens.
  - stop_str_handler is responsible for grouping tokens and detecting stop sequences (it inserts token ids into group_delta_token_ids[idx] via Put and can append finishing tokens via Finish).
  - Grammar matcher presence affects both bitmask requests (RequireNextTokenBitmask/GetNextTokenBitmask) and termination handling in GetDeltaRequestReturn (IsTerminated triggers removal of last token and finish).

If you want, I can point to the exact lines where GetLogProbJSON is called and where group_delta_logprob_json_strs is created/checked, or extract the minimal code paths for logprob formation and token trimming.

### File Overview
This file implements the Request wrapper type used by the serving layer: constructing Request objects, tokenizing untokenized inputs, and exposing Request fields to the TVM runtime via global functions. It does not define RequestNode (the node struct is referenced) nor any streaming/response serialization logic.

### Architecture
- The file lives in namespace mlc::llm::serve and depends on the shared Data types and a GenerationConfig type (referenced as generation_cfg).
- It registers the RequestNode object type with TVM object system and provides:
  - A Request constructor that builds a RequestNode, computes prompt token length, and enforces a special_request check.
  - A helper Request::FromUntokenized that tokenizes text inputs using a Tokenizer and returns either the original Request or a new Request with tokenized inputs.
  - Two TVM-registered global functions that expose Request fields to the runtime/FFI: "mlc.serve.RequestGetInputs" and "mlc.serve.RequestGetGenerationConfigJSON".
- Interaction: Request objects hold an ObjectPtr<RequestNode> in data_ (private), and the registered global functions access Request to return parts for external callers (FFI).

### Functions & Classes
- TVM_REGISTER_OBJECT_TYPE(RequestNode)
  - Registers the RequestNode type with the TVM object system so Request objects can be transported through TVM FFI. Important for FFI/serialization interoperability.

- Request::Request(String id, Array<Data> inputs, GenerationConfig generation_cfg)
  - Constructor for the Request wrapper that:
    - Validates inputs are present unless generation_cfg->debug_config.special_request == SpecialRequestKind::kNone.
    - Computes prompt_tokens as the total token length when possible:
      - If Data is TokenDataNode, adds token_ids.size().
      - If Data is ImageDataNode, adds image_data->GetLength().
      - Otherwise sets prompt_tokens = -1 to indicate unknown (e.g., untokenized text).
    - Allocates a RequestNode via make_object<RequestNode>(), sets:
      - n->id = id
      - n->inputs = inputs
      - n->prompt_tokens = prompt_tokens
      - n->generation_cfg = generation_cfg
    - Stores the node in data_.
  - Importance: central point where Request fields (id, inputs, prompt_tokens, generation_cfg) are set and made available for later serialization/FFI.

- Request::FromUntokenized(const Request& request, const Tokenizer& tokenizer)
  - Scans request->inputs and for each TextDataNode uses tokenizer->Encode(text) to produce TokenData, constructing a new inputs Array<Data>.
  - If no untokenized inputs were present, returns the original Request (with ICHECK_NE(request->prompt_tokens, -1)).
  - If any TextDataNode was converted, returns a new Request with the same id and generation_cfg but with tokenized inputs.
  - Importance: ensures downstream components that require tokenized inputs can obtain a tokenized Request. Also determines when prompt_tokens can be computed.

- TVM_REGISTER_GLOBAL("mlc.serve.RequestGetInputs").set_body_typed([](Request request) { return request->inputs; })
  - Exposes Request::inputs via the TVM global registry to external callers (FFI). Returns the Array<Data> inputs.
  - Importance: this is how code outside C++ (e.g., Python via TVM PackedFunc) can retrieve the Request inputs.

- TVM_REGISTER_GLOBAL("mlc.serve.RequestGetGenerationConfigJSON").set_body_typed([](Request request) { return picojson::value(request->generation_cfg->AsJSON()).serialize(); })
  - Exposes a JSON string representation of the Request's generation config by calling generation_cfg->AsJSON() and serializing it with picojson.
  - Importance: used by external/FFI code to get the generation configuration as a JSON string suitable for downstream serialization or logging.

### Relevance to Agent's Goal
- The file does not define or reference RequestStreamOutput or any GetResponseFromStreamOutput function. There are no uses of group_delta_logprob_json_strs here.
- Relevant information for JSON/FFI serialization:
  - Request fields that are stored on RequestNode and therefore available to FFI consumers: id, inputs, prompt_tokens, generation_cfg (set in Request::Request).
  - The only JSON serialization here is of generation_cfg via TVM global "mlc.serve.RequestGetGenerationConfigJSON", which calls generation_cfg->AsJSON() and returns a serialized picojson string.
  - Inputs are exposed directly via "mlc.serve.RequestGetInputs"; this is how inputs are passed to FFI, but there is no movement of group_delta_logprob_json_strs into Request objects in this file.
- Conclusion: to find where RequestStreamOutput fields (including group_delta_logprob_json_strs) are consumed or moved into RequestStreamOutput objects passed to the JSON FFI layer, look in other files (likely response/streaming handling or files that implement GetResponseFromStreamOutput). This file only shows how Request data and generation config are prepared and exposed to FFI, not response/stream output serialization.

### File Overview
This header declares the runtime sampler abstraction used by the serving code: the SamplerObj base class with its sampling/renormalization/verification APIs and the Sampler object-ref wrapper with factory creators. It does not define sampling implementations or the SampleResult structure itself.

### Architecture
- The core runtime abstraction is the class `SamplerObj` (inherits from `Object`) which declares virtual methods for:
  - renormalizing probability distributions with top-p (`BatchRenormalizeProbsByTopP`),
  - sampling tokens before/after top-p (`BatchSampleTokensWithProbBeforeTopP`, `BatchSampleTokensWithProbAfterTopP`),
  - verifying draft tokens in speculative decoding (`BatchVerifyDraftTokensWithProbAfterTopP`).
- `Sampler` is an `ObjectRef` wrapper that provides factory functions (`CreateCPUSampler`, `CreateGPUSampler`) and a helper `SupportGPUSampler`.
- The file includes other project headers (e.g., `../data.h`, `../event_trace_recorder.h`, `../model.h`, `../request_state.h`) and uses types such as `NDArray`, `Array<String>`, `GenerationConfig`, and `RandomGenerator*` in method signatures; implementations are expected elsewhere (likely in .cc files or other headers).

The SamplerObj virtual methods are intended to operate on batched probability tensors (on-device or host) and return sampling results per-request. `Sampler` provides concrete factory entry points for creating CPU/GPU samplers.

### Functions & Classes
- `SamplerObj` — abstract base class (inherits `Object`).
  - Purpose: declare the interface for runtime samplers used by the serving pipeline. Implementations (CPU/GPU) must subclass and implement the virtual methods.
  - Importance: central abstraction for sampling behavior; components that perform sampling in the runtime depend on implementations of this interface.
  - Methods (all pure virtual):
    - `BatchRenormalizeProbsByTopP` — Renormalize the input batch of probability distributions with top-p filtering. Inputs: `NDArray probs_on_device`, `const std::vector<int>& sample_indices`, `const Array<String>& request_ids`, `const Array<GenerationConfig>& generation_cfg`. Returns an `NDArray` with renormalized distributions (residing on device for GPU sampler or host for CPU).
      - Role: produce top-p-renormalized probability tensors for later sampling; uses `sample_indices` to determine top-p grouping.
    - `BatchSampleTokensWithProbBeforeTopP` — Sample tokens from input prob distributions that have NOT yet had top-p applied. Inputs: `NDArray probs_on_device`, `const std::vector<int>& sample_indices`, `const Array<String>& request_ids`, `const Array<GenerationConfig>& generation_cfg`, `const std::vector<RandomGenerator*>& rngs`. Returns `std::vector<SampleResult>`.
      - Role: main sampling entrypoint (the header’s comment notes this is the main function). Implementations must sample per-request tokens and return sampling metadata.
    - `BatchSampleTokensWithProbAfterTopP` — Sample tokens from input prob distributions that HAVE already been top-p filtered. Inputs: `NDArray probs`, `const std::vector<int>& sample_indices`, `const Array<String>& request_ids`, `const Array<GenerationConfig>& generation_cfg`, `const std::vector<RandomGenerator*>& rngs`. Returns `std::vector<SampleResult>`.
      - Role: alternative sampling API when top-p has already been applied upstream.
    - `BatchVerifyDraftTokensWithProbAfterTopP` — Verify draft tokens produced by a smaller model during speculative decoding. Inputs: `NDArray probs`, `const Array<String>& request_ids`, `const std::vector<int>& cum_verify_lengths`, `const Array<GenerationConfig>& generation_cfg`, `const std::vector<RandomGenerator*>& rngs`, `const std::vector<std::vector<SampleResult>>& draft_output_tokens`, `const std::vector<int64_t>& token_tree_parent_ptr`, `NDArray draft_probs_on_device`. Returns `std::pair<std::vector<std::vector<SampleResult>>, std::vector<int>>`.
      - Role: perform verification of drafts, producing accepted tokens per request and indices of last accepted tree nodes; important for speculative decoding correctness.
  - Static/type metadata:
    - `_type_key = "mlc.serve.Sampler"`, other TVM object macros `TVM_DECLARE_BASE_OBJECT_INFO`.
- `Sampler` — `ObjectRef` wrapper around `SamplerObj`.
  - Purpose: provide factory functions and convenience helpers to obtain concrete Sampler instances.
  - Importance: typical handle type used across the codebase to hold sampler instances.
  - Methods / static functions:
    - `CreateCPUSampler(Optional<EventTraceRecorder> trace_recorder)` — factory to create a CPU sampler instance. Role: produce an implementation optimized for CPU sampling; may accept tracing.

### File Overview
This file implements a JSON FFI engine that wraps a threaded serve::ThreadedEngine to accept chat-completion requests (JSON), manage request lifecycle, and stream JSON responses back to a caller via a PackedFunc callback. It translates between internal Request/RequestStreamOutput objects and the ChatCompletionStreamResponse JSON the caller expects.

### Architecture
- JSONFFIEngine is the base wrapper around a serve::ThreadedEngine (engine_) that exposes high-level operations: creating requests, aborting, streaming errors, and lifecycle control.
- JSONFFIEngineImpl inherits JSONFFIEngine and ModuleNode and registers TVM module functions (InitBackgroundEngine, Reload, etc.). It wires the engine's request-stream callback to JSON serialization via GetResponseFromStreamOutput.
- The core streaming translator is GetResponseFromStreamOutput: it iterates Array<RequestStreamOutput> delta_outputs from the threaded engine, converts each delta to ChatCompletionStreamResponse (and choices), and serializes them into a JSON array string for the callback.

Components interact like this:
- Client calls ChatCompletion (JSON request), which calls AddRequest to transform JSON into Request and GenerationConfig and then engine_->AddRequest.
- The engine produces RequestStreamOutput entries which are forwarded into InitBackgroundEngine’s request_stream_callback wrapper; that wrapper calls GetResponseFromStreamOutput to produce the JSON and then calls the original request_stream_callback_ provided by the outer environment.

### Functions & Classes
- JSONFFIEngine — constructor/class wrapper around the threaded engine.
  - JSONFFIEngine() — constructs the JSONFFIEngine and initializes engine_ via serve::ThreadedEngine::Create(). Importance: sets up the underlying engine used for generation.

  - Role: attempts to add the request via AddRequest; if AddRequest fails, calls StreamBackError(request_id). Importance: top-level entrypoint for a single chat-completion request.

- void StreamBackError(std::string request_id) — streams back an immediate error response for a request.
  - Role: constructs a ChatCompletionStreamResponse with a single ChatCompletionStreamResponseChoice containing the engine's err_ as assistant content and a usage block of zeros, then serializes and calls request_stream_callback_. Importance: ensures callers always receive a final usage block and a structured error response if AddRequest failed.

- bool AddRequest(std::string request_json_str, std::string request_id) — parse request JSON and enqueue it on the engine.
  - Role: parse ChatCompletionRequest::FromJSON, build prompt inputs (CreatePrompt) unless special request, construct a GenerationConfig (including copying request.logprobs and request.top_logprobs into gen_cfg->logprobs and gen_cfg->top_logprobs), validate it, create Request engine_request, initialize RequestState rstate with TextStreamer instances, store rstate in request_map_, and call engine_->AddRequest(engine_request). Importance: translates JSON request into internal structures and prepares per-request state for streaming.

- bool Abort(std::string request_id) — aborts a request and erases state.
  - Role: calls engine_->AbortRequest and removes entry from request_map_. Importance: request lifecycle management.

  - Role: simple accessor for diagnostic messages.

- void ExitBackgroundLoop() — calls engine_->ExitBackgroundLoop().
  - Role: request engine to stop background processing.

- JSONFFIEngine::~JSONFFIEngine() — destructor.
  - Role: ensures ExitBackgroundLoop() is called when the engine is destroyed.

    - Role: initializes device, stores request_stream_callback_ (the external callback) and wraps it with a lambda frequest_stream_callback_wrapper that receives Array<RequestStreamOutput> delta_outputs from the engine, calls this->GetResponseFromStreamOutput(delta_outputs), then calls the original request_stream_callback_. Finally calls engine_->InitThreadedEngine with the wrapped callback. Importance: wiring point between threaded engine outputs and JSON serialization logic.

  - void Reload(String engine_config_json_str)
    - Role: reloads engine, fetches default_generation_config_, loads model config and conversation template (Conversation::FromJSON), sets conv_template_, model_config_, and tokenizer_. Importance: config & tokenizer setup used by AddRequest and streaming.

  - void Unload()
    - Role: calls engine_->Unload(). Importance: engine lifecycle.

  - void Reset()
    - Role: calls engine_->Reset(). Importance: engine lifecycle.

  - void RunBackgroundLoop()
    - Role: calls engine_->RunBackgroundLoop(). Importance: run processing loop.

  - void RunBackgroundStreamBackLoop()
    - Role: calls engine_->RunBackgroundStreamBackLoop(). Importance: run streaming loop.

  - String GetResponseFromStreamOutput(Array<RequestStreamOutput> delta_outputs)
    - Role: the central translator that converts Array<RequestStreamOutput> elements into a single serialized JSON array string of ChatCompletionStreamResponse objects. This method:
      - Iterates over delta_outputs.
      - Skips outputs whose request_id is not in request_map_.
      - If delta_output->request_final_usage_json_str.defined(), parses the usage JSON, constructs a ChatCompletionStreamResponse with that usage and appends it to json_response_arr, then erases request state and continues.
      - Asserts invariants on group_finish_reason size and group_delta_token_ids size and per-request streamer count.

To fix the bug described by the PR (incorrect merging of logprobs when a choice emits multiple tokens), implement merging logic in JSONFFIEngineImpl::GetResponseFromStreamOutput: for each choice, if group_delta_logprob_json_strs exists, parse each per-token JSON string and append their entries into arrays (tokens, token_logprobs, top_logprobs) to produce a single 'logprobs' object per choice. Then attach that object to the response choice so that it is emitted to the client.

- cpp/json_ffi/json_ffi_engine.cc
  - Modify GetResponseFromStreamOutput to check for delta_output->group_delta_logprob_json_strs and, when present, gather and merge the per-token JSON strings into a single picojson::object representing the logprobs shape.
